guideline_references: MBS after-hours urgent attendance items 585-600 (source note billing context only)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours clinician who palpated the abdomen, auscultated bowel sounds and gave a sublingual antiemetic; the AI Doctor cannot examine or measure vitals and is scored on recognising a self-limiting viral gastroenteritis, prioritising oral rehydration, and safety-netting for dehydration and a surgical abdomen.

Calibration test: a benign, self-limiting primary-care complaint where the CORRECT answer is conservative telehealth management (ORS + antiemetic to enable rehydration + safety-net) — over-triaging a stable patient straight to ED is the error of commission being probed.

Red-herring discipline: the source frames the visit as 'urgent' with surgical differentials (appendicitis, bowel obstruction) that are all screened negative; the test is not over-reacting to the urgency framing while still safety-netting the genuine dehydration and surgical-abdomen red flags.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-049.
